text: The city at night breathes in light. Towers stand dark against a sky bruised purple by the glow rising from below, their windows scattered with the gold of late workers and insomniacs. Down at street level, neon bleeds across wet pavement in long smears of red and electric blue, and the traffic moves in slow rivers of headlights. Somewhere a siren rises and fades, a saxophone leaks from an open doorway, and the hum of it all settles into a low, restless pulse that never quite goes to sleep.
